title: Gomphia guianensis
taxonomy: Gomphia guianensis is classified in the family Ochnaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the Ochna family. This family is part of the order Ochnales and is native to tropical regions of Africa and the Americas. The species Gomphia guianensis is a member of the genus Gomphia, which includes about 20 species of trees and shrubs. The Gomphia genus is further divided into several subgenera, with Gomphia guianensis being part of the subgenus Gomphia. The classification of Gomphia guianensis reflects its unique characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Ochnaceae family.

The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties.
